campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships in the city. They also discussed extending a temporary local law and moving forward with plans to build a bike and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including submitting grant applications to help pay for it.
keyTopics: $4,000 donation to youth recreation scholarships from Water Tower Kitchen
Extension of interim ordinance #2326 (temporary local law)
Hamilton Avenue bike/pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 planning and grant applications
Final feasibility study approval for the Highway 17 overcrossing project

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public meeting on March 18, 2026 where residents had a chance to speak about a new rule or policy. After listening to public comments, the council introduced a new ordinance (a local law) and decided to skip the usual first-reading waiting period so they could vote on it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
New ordinance introduced and expedited for faster approval
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular council meeting
Council waived standard first-reading procedure

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various sessions held in March and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily focused on administrative housekeeping and moving forward with a construction project, though specific details about the project were not fully listed in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple council sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Review of closed session discussions from March
Town Manager given authority to execute construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 21, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve several important agreements and projects. Main topics included updating the airport's baggage system contract, protecting affordable housing if subsidies end, hiring a consultant for storm sewer planning, and moving forward with a FEMA-funded family camp project.
keyTopics: Airport baggage handling system upgrade contract
Affordable housing protections for low-income residents
Storm sewer system city-wide planning
FEMA family camp project implementation

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting to discuss the city's spending plans for the next fiscal year, including what money will go toward infrastructure projects like roads, parks, and buildings. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to honor young people and community volunteers in the city.
keyTopics: Next year's capital improvement budget and which city projects will be funded
Teen Month proclamation recognizing local youth
National Volunteer Week recognition for community volunteers

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, starting with the Pledge of Allegiance. The council highlighted two important observances: Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month, recognizing these communities and issues affecting local residents.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month awareness
Regular city council business and updates

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a community budget workshop to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows how the city spends money. The council also reviewed rules about lobbyists—people who try to influence city decisions—and when they need to register their activities with the city.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool training for residents
City budget transparency and public engagement
Lobbyist registration and disclosure requirements
Rules for people influencing city legislative or administrative decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal information for residents about challenging city decisions. If you disagree with a city decision, you have 90 days from when that decision becomes final to file a lawsuit or legal challenge.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to challenge city decisions in court
Legal rights for residents disputing quasi-adjudicative decisions
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to discuss updates to the city's long-term housing and community development plan (2025-2030), review requests from individual council members for new work projects, and consider changing the rules for how council vacancies are filled. The meeting also included routine items like approving previous meeting notes and scheduling upcoming meetings.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New work project requests from individual council members
Proposed changes to how council vacancies are handled
Approval of previous meeting minutes and future meeting calendar

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a historic building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner wants the building officially recognized as more historically important, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new space as a bonus for fixing up the building and making it earthquake-safe.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade (Category 4 to Category 2)
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic and historic renovations
Downtown Commercial zone development
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
